---
description: "Use when setting up, configuring, or maintaining the Docusaurus v3 project. Covers docusaurus.config.ts setup, _category_.json format, sidebar auto-generation, plugin installation (search, Mermaid), admonition syntax, dev commands, and domain position assignments. Auto-applied to config and category files."
applyTo: "docusaurus.config.ts, sidebars.ts, **/_category_.json, package.json"
---

# Docusaurus Project Guidelines

This project uses **Docusaurus v3** with the `@docusaurus/preset-classic` preset.

---

## Bootstrap a New Project

```bash
npx create-docusaurus@latest <project-name> classic
# TypeScript variant:
npx create-docusaurus@latest <project-name> classic --typescript
```

---

## Required Plugins & Packages

```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "@docusaurus/core": "^3.x",
    "@docusaurus/preset-classic": "^3.x",
    "@docusaurus/theme-mermaid": "^3.x"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@easyops-cn/docusaurus-search-local": "^0.x"
  }
}
```

---

## `docusaurus.config.ts` — Minimum Required Setup

```js
// @ts-check
const { themes } = require('prism-react-renderer');

/** @type {import('@docusaurus/types').Config} */
const config = {
  title: 'Java & Spring Boot Notes',
  tagline: 'Interview prep & knowledge base for Java backend engineers',
  url: 'https://your-domain.com',
  baseUrl: '/',
  onBrokenLinks: 'throw',         // ← fail build on broken internal links
  onBrokenMarkdownLinks: 'warn',
  favicon: 'img/favicon.ico',
  organizationName: 'your-github-username',
  projectName: 'exp-java-ai',

  markdown: {
    mermaid: true,                // ← enable Mermaid diagram rendering
  },

  presets: [
    [
      'classic',
      {
        docs: {
          sidebarPath: require.resolve('./sidebars.ts'),
          editUrl: 'https://github.com/your-username/exp-java-ai/edit/main/',
          showLastUpdateTime: true,    // ← shows last_updated from frontmatter
          showLastUpdateAuthor: false,
          tagsBasePath: 'tags',
        },
        blog: false,                   // ← disable blog unless needed
        theme: {
          customCss: require.resolve('./src/css/custom.css'),
        },
      },
    ],
  ],

  themes: [
    '@docusaurus/theme-mermaid',   // ← required for Mermaid diagrams
    [
      '@easyops-cn/docusaurus-search-local',
      {
        hashed: true,
        language: ['en'],
        highlightSearchTermsOnTargetPage: true,
        explicitSearchResultPath: true,
      },
    ],
  ],

  themeConfig: {
    navbar: {
      title: 'Java Notes',
      logo: { alt: 'Java Notes', src: 'img/logo.svg' },
      items: [
        { type: 'docSidebar', sidebarId: 'tutorialSidebar', position: 'left', label: 'Docs' },
        { href: 'https://github.com/your-username/exp-java-ai', label: 'GitHub', position: 'right' },
      ],
    },
    prism: {
      theme: themes.github,
      darkTheme: themes.dracula,
      additionalLanguages: ['java', 'bash', 'yaml', 'json', 'xml'],  // ← add Java syntax highlighting
    },
  },
};

module.exports = config;
```

---

## `sidebars.ts` — Auto-Generated Sidebar

Always use auto-generation. Do NOT hardcode sidebar items.

```js
/** @type {import('@docusaurus/plugin-content-docs').SidebarsConfig} */
const sidebars = {
  tutorialSidebar: [{ type: 'autogenerated', dirName: '.' }],
};

module.exports = sidebars;
```

Sidebar sort order is controlled by:
- **`sidebar_position`** in note frontmatter (for individual pages)
- **`position`** in `_category_.json` (for folders/categories)

---

## `_category_.json` — Required in Every Domain Folder

Every `docs/<domain>/` folder **must** have a `_category_.json`. No exceptions.

### With `index.md` (preferred)

```json
{
  "label": "Human-Readable Category Name",
  "position": 5,
  "link": {
    "type": "doc",
    "id": "<domain>-index"
  }
}
```

- `label`: Text displayed in the sidebar
- `position`: Integer; lower = higher in sidebar
- `link.id`: The `id` field from the folder's `index.md` frontmatter

### Without `index.md` (fallback)

```json
{
  "label": "Category Name",
  "position": 5,
  "link": { "type": "generated-index" }
}
```

---

## Admonitions

Use Docusaurus admonitions for key callouts in notes:

```markdown
:::tip
Best practice shortcut — something most developers don't know.
:::

:::info
Background context — useful but not in the critical path.
:::

:::warning
Common mistake — easy to get wrong.
:::

:::danger
Destructive or security-sensitive behavior — proceed carefully.
:::
```

---

## Domain Sidebar Positions

| Position | Domain | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| 1 | `overviews` | Quick-reference summaries |
| 2 | `core-java` | Language basics |
| 3 | `oops` | OOP principles |
| 4 | `java-type-system` | Generics, autoboxing, type erasure |
| 5 | `core-apis` | Object, String, Math, wrappers |
| 6 | `collections-framework` | Collections hierarchy and utilities |
| 7 | `exceptions` | Exception hierarchy and handling |
| 8 | `functional-programming` | Lambdas, Streams, method references |
| 9 | `multithreading` | Threads, concurrency, virtual threads |
| 10 | `io` | File handling, NIO, serialization |
| 11 | `jvm-internals` | Memory, GC, class loading, JIT |
| 12 | `annotations` | Built-in, custom, meta-annotations |
| 13 | `modules` | Java 9+ module system |
| 14 | `java-evolution` | New features by Java version |
| 15 | `java-design-patterns` | GoF patterns in Java |
| 16 | `DSA` | Data structures and algorithms |
| 17 | `spring-framework` | Core Spring: IoC, AOP, context |
| 18 | `spring-boot` | Auto-config, starters, beans, DI |
| 19 | `spring-data` | JPA, repositories, transactions |
| 20 | `spring-security` | Auth, OAuth2, JWT, filters |
| 21 | `web` | REST, HTTP, MVC, WebFlux |
| 22 | `messaging` | Kafka, RabbitMQ, async patterns |
| 23 | `databases` | SQL, NoSQL, migrations |
| 24 | `testing` | Unit, integration, Testcontainers |
| 25 | `build-tools` | Maven, Gradle |
| 26 | `version-control` | Git internals, branching |
| 27 | `docker` | Containerization, Compose |
| 28 | `kubernetes` | Pods, services, deployments |
| 29 | `cloud` | AWS/GCP/Azure patterns |
| 30 | `devops` | CI/CD, observability |
| 31 | `system-design` | Architecture, microservices |
| 32 | `java-cheatsheets` | Quick reference pages |
| 33 | `interview-prep` | Consolidated Q&A |

---

## Dev Commands

```bash
npm start              # Start dev server at http://localhost:3000
npm run build          # Production build (runs broken-link checks)
npm run serve          # Serve the production build locally
npm run clear          # Clear Docusaurus cache — use when styles/diagrams don't update
```

---

## Constraints

- Always use **Docusaurus v3** APIs — do NOT use v2 patterns or v2 plugin names
- `onBrokenLinks: 'throw'` must stay — do not downgrade to `'warn'`
- `showLastUpdateTime: true` must remain enabled
- Sidebar is always auto-generated — do NOT hardcode items
- `_category_.json` `link.id` **must** match the `id` in the corresponding `index.md`
- Do NOT enable the blog plugin unless explicitly requested

---

## Demo Pages — Hidden from Sidebar

Demo pages (`docs/<domain>/demo/*.md`) provide hands-on code walkthroughs hidden from the main sidebar. Set them up as follows:

### 1. `demo/_category_.json`

Create this file in every `demo/` subfolder — no `label` or `position` needed:

```json
{
  "className": "hidden"
}
```

### 2. CSS Rule in `src/css/custom.css`

Add this rule **once** — it applies to all `demo/` folders site-wide:

```css
/* Hide demo pages from sidebar navigation */
.menu__list .hidden > .menu__list-item-collapsible,
.menu__list .hidden {
  display: none;
}
```

### 3. Demo Page Frontmatter Requirements

Every demo page must have these two frontmatter fields to remove prev/next navigation arrows:

```yaml
pagination_next: null
pagination_prev: null
```

### 4. Linking from Topic Notes

Link to demo pages from the corresponding topic note using a `:::tip` admonition. Place it after the opening tagline or inside `## Code Examples`:

```markdown
:::tip Practical Demo
Runnable step-by-step examples: [<Topic> Demo](./demo/<topic-id>-demo.md)
:::
```

---

## Code Blocks — Line Numbers & Highlighting

### Enable Line Numbers per Block

Add `showLineNumbers` after the language identifier:

````
```java showLineNumbers
public class Example {}
```
````

### Highlight Specific Lines

Add `{line-numbers}` after the language identifier:

````
```java showLineNumbers {3,7-9}
// line 3 and lines 7–9 will be highlighted
```
````

### Add a Filename as Block Title

````
```java title="ThreadExample.java" showLineNumbers {2,5-7}
// code here
```
````

### Output Blocks

Follow every code block in a demo page with the expected output:

````
**Expected Output:**
```
output text here
```
````

> **Convention**: Use `showLineNumbers` in demo pages as standard. In topic notes, use it only when discussing specific line logic.
